title: orobanche spartii var. lutea
intro: Orobanche spartii var. lutea, also known as Yellow Broomrape, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of the broom bush (Spartium junceum) and other related species. Traditionally, Orobanche spartii var. lutea has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been employed to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive problems, and supporting the immune system. Orobanche spartii var. lutea is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in certain regions.
